# Login with SeaTalk Overview

**Source:** https://open.seatalk.io/docs/login-with-seatalk-overview

Please note that the minimum mobile client version required for Login with SeaTalk is 3.17.

Login with SeaTalk helps you quickly and easily manage sign-in and user authentication on your website. To let the users of your app log into your website using SeaTalk, all you need to do is embed an HTML code snippet into your website to show the Login with SeaTalk button. After the user confirms the login, your app will get the key SeaTalk profile information of the user.

Compared to other Single Sign-On tools, Login with SeaTalk provides the following benefits:

- Getting the key work profile information after the user authenticates the login

- Getting the full work profile of a user and other related organisation information by calling SeaTalk Open APIs

- Delivering a smooth and secure user experience for your users

This overview introduces when to use Login with SeaTalk, the available login methods and what you can get from successful login authentication.

## When to Use Login With SeaTalk

Login with SeaTalk can meet your needs if:

- You seek an SSO tool with low deployment efforts

- You want to provide a safe and smooth login experience for your users

- You want to use other open capabilities provided by SeaTalk Open Platform, such as Open APIs

## Three Login Methods

Login with SeaTalk supports three login methods. They correspond to three different security and convenience levels to suit your particular scenarios:

- One-Tap: users tap a button to log in seamlessly

- Confirm on Phone: users receive a notification on their mobile SeaTalk app to confirm the login

- QR-code: users use their mobile SeaTalk app to scan a QR-code for login

![x0WNQm5UdQAAAABJRU5ErkJggg==](data:image/png;base64,)

Note:

- Currently, only websites support Login With SeaTalk

## Get User Identity After Authentication

SeaTalk Open Platform will provide the basic employee information upon successful user identity authentication:

- employee_code, which is the unique identifier of an employee within an organisation

- Profile picture

- Name

- Email or phone number

## Ready to Start?

Login with SeaTalk is one of the SeaTalk open platform app's capabilities, so to tap into this capability you need to create a SeaTalk Open Platform app to configure Login with SeaTalk. To learn more about how to create and configure an app, see Build an App For Your Team (../../../../../../docs/build-an-app-for-your-team).

The diagram below shows the mechanism of how Login with SeaTalk works. If you are ready to begin to implement this on your website, you can get started here (../../../../../../docs/login-with-seatalk-get-started).
